acceptance_criteria: Spreadsheet formulas and computed columns are programmatically verifiable (CSV/formulas export present and consistent with memo).
Memo preview is complete (no truncation) and mitigation table labels/numbers are consistent with spreadsheet exports.
All numeric inconsistencies (notably S4) are resolved and documented with per-shot reconciliation.
Grader score provenance note added explaining past deduction or confirming resolution (if resolved, final score should be updated).
priority_items_order: Provide spreadsheet formula exports / re-generate XLSX
Fix numeric/label inconsistency and add per-shot reconciliation
Document equality semantics and score provenance
recommended_reviewers: independent verifier (to confirm spreadsheet parsing and reconciliation)

planned_changes: Re-open or re-generate disruption_timing_review.xlsx in an environment with Excel-reading libraries (openpyxl or xlsxwriter) and attach: (a) CSV export of all computed columns used in decision logic, (b) a cell-formulas export (text/CSV) for all formula-bearing cells referenced in the memo, and (c) screenshots of the formula bar for any complex/opaque formulas.
Update operations_gate_memo.md to include the full (non-truncated) content in the artifact, ensuring the mitigation-outcomes decision table is complete and visible in previews.

Correct the mitigation-outcomes table labeling and numeric entries (S4) and add a per-shot reconciliation block that shows baseline values, transformation steps, and final computed results for each mitigation so values are unambiguous.
Explicitly state comparison semantics used (>= vs >) for timing_ok, mass_ok, and lockout rules and reflect those exact boolean expressions both in memo and in exported spreadsheet formulas.

Add a short grader-note section in the memo metadata or separate file that documents any prior deductions (e.g., the 0.05 deduction) and lists remaining unresolved verification risks (if any) and how they were resolved in this revision.
Run a quick self-check script (included as a small .py file or documented commands) that reads the exported CSVs and reproduces the memo's intermediate tables to demonstrate reproducibility.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Resolve verification blockers (unverifiable XLSX formulas, truncated memo preview, and numeric/label inconsistency) so deterministic and semantic checks can pass and verifiers can reproduce results.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline submission includes the required memo and an XLSX produced via manual OpenXML and captures the documented rules and per-shot calculations. Primary outstanding issues: (1) inability to programmatically preview/verify the .xlsx (openpyxl unavailable) leaves formulas unverified; (2) operations_gate_memo.md preview is truncated preventing full inspection; (3) a high-severity numeric/label inconsistency in the memo mitigation-outcomes table for S4 (memo baseline shows trigger_margin_ms = -1, mitigation table shows inconsistent '8 / 8 / TRUE / TRUE'); and (4) minor reporting/score provenance clarity is needed (explain the 0.05 deduction). Resolve these to enable deterministic verification and remove ambiguity in decision tables.
